questions.question: Consider a pandas DataFrame with a mix of numeric and categorical data. Explain the difference between the `df.isnull()` and `df.isna()` methods. How do these methods handle missing data in the DataFrame? (10 marks)

Write a Python code snippet to create a new column in a pandas DataFrame that contains the square of each value in the 'Age' column. Use the `df.assign()` method or the `df.map()` method to achieve this. (8 marks)

Suppose you have a pandas DataFrame with a 'Country' column that contains categorical data. You want to perform a groupby operation on this column, but some countries have missing values. How would you handle this situation using the `groupby()` method and the `dropna()` method? Provide a sample code snippet to demonstrate this. (12 marks)
